gaps.rationale: Senpi deliberately reports that remote control is unavailable because it has no Codex remote-control handle; Codex's locally disabled no-handle response remains server-specific.
Senpi's goal backing store supports active, paused, and complete only. Codex-only blocked, usageLimited, and budgetLimited statuses are rejected rather than fabricated.
After a process restart Senpi reconstructs user-message-only historical turns from its session log; live-turn pagination remains wire-shaped but cannot recreate Codex's persisted item detail.
The isolated Senpi cell truthfully has no Codex account and returns its honest API-key/unauthenticated posture instead of emulating Codex account state.
Senpi aggregates projected file-change unified diffs while Codex derives the diff from its git-backed turn state; notification audience and ordering remain strict.

normalizations.rationale: The normalized transcript replaces each truthful server product/version string with a server-identity marker so exact protocol comparison does not require Senpi to claim it is Codex.
